figures.caption: Table 2: Subjective evaluation scores (1–5 scale) by 30 human assessors. Bold values indicate the best results per column.
Table 4: Quantitative comparison on the full ZS330 and HC770 test sets. ↑: higher is better; ↓: lower is better. Bold and underlined values indicate best and second-best results.

Table 3: Quantitative comparison on representative images from ZS330 (no-reference only) and HC770 (all metrics). ↑: higher is better; ↓: lower is better. Bold and underlined values indicate best and secondbest results.
Table 6: Runtime comparison (in seconds) across different image resolutions. Bold values indicate the fastest method.
